01_initial_request.target_name: Lemna gibba central carbon metabolism network model
01_initial_request.input_data: Draft genome, Lemna gibba fronds, nitrate, glutamine
01_initial_request.user_intent: To establish a framework for quantitative metabolic research in duckweeds and examine metabolic adaptation to different nutritional conditions
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Quantification of growth kinetics, metabolite levels, transcript abundance, and metabolic fluxes

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Duckweeds are promising model organisms for metabolic studies but require defined growth conditions. [Gap] Need to establish growth kinetics under different nitrogen sources to compare metabolic adaptation. [Decision] To achieve quantitative growth comparison, chose to use Liquid Culture System (SH Medium) to perform axenic cultivation of Lemna gibba fronds on nitrate or glutamine, expecting to obtain exponential growth curves.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Cultures grew in an exponential fashion; INS growth rate 0.014 h⁻¹, ONS growth rate 0.019 h⁻¹.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Cultures are growing but growth rates need precise quantification. [Gap] Manual measurement is imprecise for high-throughput kinetics. [Decision] To achieve accurate growth kinetics, chose to use Scanalyzer BS with LemnaLauncher software to perform frond surface area measurement, expecting to obtain exponential trend lines and specific growth rates.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Growth rates calculated: INS 0.014±0.001 h⁻¹, ONS 0.019±0.001 h⁻¹ (1.4-fold faster).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Growth rates are known but biomass composition determines metabolic demand. [Gap] Need to fractionate biomass to analyze specific components. [Decision] To achieve biomass composition analysis, chose to use Organic biphasic solvent extraction procedure to perform fractionation into lipid, polar metabolites, and cell pellet, expecting to obtain dry weight fractions of each component.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Biomass fractions obtained: lipid, free metabolites, cell pellet (insoluble material).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Biomass is fractionated but protein and cell wall content are unknown. [Gap] Need to quantify nitrogen and structural carbohydrates. [Decision] To achieve protein and cell wall quantification, chose to use vario MICRO cube (CHN Elemental Analyzer) to perform total nitrogen measurement, expecting to obtain protein weight based on amino acid composition.

parameters.conversion: Nitrogen to protein weight based on duckweed protein composition

02_agent_trajectory.observation: ONS fronds had 40% more protein and half the cell wall fraction compared to INS.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Protein is quantified but starch content remains unknown. [Gap] Need to measure storage carbohydrates. [Decision] To achieve starch quantification, chose to use Glucose oxidase reagent to perform enzymatic assay on digested starch, expecting to obtain starch content percentage.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Starch content determined as part of cell pellet fraction.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Carbohydrates are measured but lipid composition is unknown. [Gap] Need to profile fatty acids for metabolic modeling. [Decision] To achieve fatty acid profiling, chose to use Agilent 6890N GC-MS to perform analysis of fatty acid methyl esters (FAMEs), expecting to obtain relative abundance of fatty acids.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Palmitic, linoleic, and α-linolenic acid comprised ~90% of total fatty acids.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Lipids are profiled but free metabolite levels are unknown. [Gap] Need to quantify central metabolites for flux analysis constraints. [Decision] To achieve metabolite profiling, chose to use ABSciex Triple Quadrupole MS (LC-MS) to perform HILIC analysis of polar metabolites, expecting to obtain concentrations of 57 metabolites.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Citrate and malate significantly increased in ONS condition.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Biomass composition is known but substrate uptake rates are needed for flux balance. [Gap] Need to measure glucose and Gln depletion. [Decision] To achieve substrate uptake measurement, chose to use 4500 QTRAP Mass Spectrometer to perform MRM analysis of medium substrates, expecting to obtain uptake fluxes.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Glucose uptake reduced under ONS; Gln uptake determined.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Uptake rates are measured but intracellular fluxes require isotopic tracing. [Gap] Need to introduce isotopic label into metabolism. [Decision] To achieve metabolic flux analysis, chose to use Isotopic Labeling (Medium) to perform cultivation with 13C-glucose, expecting to obtain labeled biomass for isotopomer analysis.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Labeled frond biomass harvested for GC/MS and EA-IRMS.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Labeled biomass is available but mass isotopomer distributions are unknown. [Gap] Need to measure labeling patterns in metabolites. [Decision] To achieve isotopomer measurement, chose to use Agilent 6890N/5975 GC-MS to perform SIM analysis of derivatized metabolites, expecting to obtain mass isotopomer composition for 16 analytes.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 13C-enrichment data obtained; ONS showed lower enrichment due to unlabeled Gln.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Metabolite labeling is measured but total biomass carbon enrichment is needed for CO2 uptake calculation. [Gap] Need bulk 13C enrichment value. [Decision] To achieve total biomass 13C measurement, chose to use PDZ Europa EA-IRMS to perform elemental analysis, expecting to obtain fractional 13C enrichment in total biomass carbon.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Biomass carbon enriched at 21.1% (INS) indicating atmospheric CO2 fixation.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Metabolic data is collected but transcriptional regulation is unknown. [Gap] Need to assess gene expression changes. [Decision] To achieve transcriptome profiling, chose to use Illumina HiSeq 2000 to perform RNA sequencing, expecting to obtain read counts for 21,830 predicted transcripts.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: RNA-seq libraries generated and sequenced.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Sequencing reads are generated but need to be mapped to the genome. [Gap] Need to quantify transcript abundance. [Decision] To achieve read mapping, chose to use Bowtie to perform alignment against Lemna gibba draft genome, expecting to obtain mapped read counts per gene.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Reads mapped against predicted transcripts.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Reads are mapped but differential expression is unknown. [Gap] Need to identify genes changing between conditions. [Decision] To achieve differential expression analysis, chose to use DESeq package to perform statistical testing, expecting to obtain list of significantly differentially expressed genes.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 307 genes found differentially expressed (34 up, 273 down in ONS).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] RNA-seq results are obtained but require validation. [Gap] Need to confirm expression changes of key genes. [Decision] To achieve validation, chose to use Bio-Rad CFX96 Touch Real-time PCR Detection System to perform qRT-PCR, expecting to obtain consistent direction of change for selected genes.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: qRT-PCR data consistent with RNA-seq direction of change.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] DE genes are identified but functional context is unknown. [Gap] Need to understand biological processes affected. [Decision] To achieve functional annotation, chose to use DAVID web tool to perform GO term enrichment analysis, expecting to obtain enriched biological processes.

parameters.background: Uniprot homologs of L. gibba proteins

02_agent_trajectory.observation: GO terms pointed towards redox processes and peroxidases.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Omics data is collected but a metabolic network is needed for flux analysis. [Gap] Need to predict enzyme functions from genome. [Decision] To achieve network construction, chose to use Ensemble Enzyme Prediction Pipeline (E2P2) to perform enzyme annotation, expecting to obtain predicted enzyme functions for gene loci.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Enzyme functions predicted and mapped to central carbon metabolism network.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Network is constructed but model configuration for CO2 uptake is uncertain. [Gap] Need to determine correct model topology for flux fitting. [Decision] To achieve model validation, chose to use 13CFLUX2 to perform testing of different model configurations (CO2 pools), expecting to obtain goodness-of-fit measures (SSR).

parameters.configurations_tested: 3 (Net efflux only, CO2_RBC pool, CO2_RBC + uptake rates)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Configuration with CO2 uptake and exchange passed goodness-of-fit test (SSR 48.0 INS, 19.9 ONS).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Best model configuration is selected but flux values and uncertainties are needed. [Gap] Need to estimate net fluxes and confidence intervals. [Decision] To achieve flux estimation, chose to use 13CFLUX2 to perform iterative flux parameter fitting and Monte Carlo simulation, expecting to obtain net flux values with statistical confidence.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Flux values obtained for 89 net fluxes; 49 differed significantly between conditions.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Flux estimation is done with 13CFLUX2 but parts of analysis used another tool. [Gap] Need to verify or supplement analysis. [Decision] To achieve parts of the analysis, chose to use influx_s to perform metabolic flux analysis, expecting to obtain consistent flux results.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Analysis performed alongside 13CFLUX2.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Metabolic data suggests CO2 fixation but cellular mechanism is unclear. [Gap] Need to visualize chloroplast localization to explain CO2 access. [Decision] To achieve cellular imaging, chose to use Zeiss LSM 780 CLSM to perform confocal microscopy of mesophyll cells, expecting to obtain chloroplast distribution images.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Chloroplasts densely packed against cell walls facing intercellular air spaces.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Integrated metabolic, transcriptomic and flux analysis

protein_content_increase.interpretation: Higher protein accumulation in ONS compared to INS

differentially_expressed_genes.interpretation: Significant transcriptional changes between nitrogen sources

model_goodness_of_fit_INS.interpretation: Acceptable fit for inorganic nitrogen condition

model_goodness_of_fit_ONS.interpretation: Acceptable fit for organic nitrogen condition

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was achieved. A central carbon metabolism network model for Lemna gibba was established based on its draft genome. Growth kinetics, metabolite levels, transcript abundance, and metabolic fluxes were quantified under nitrate and glutamine conditions, revealing substantial reorganization of TCA cycle fluxes and a disconnect between gene expression and metabolic flux.
